block_03: In January-February, the Asian world celebrates one of its biggest holidays — Lunar New Year. Don’t miss a great incentive to approach the segment of users that celebrates it!
block_05: Happy Lunar New Year, Chinese New Year, or Spring Festival? 🤔
block_06: The terms "Chinese New Year," "Lunar New Year," and "Spring Festival" are often mentioned in the same context, but they are not the same thing.
block_07: Chinese New Year is based on the Chinese lunar calendar and kicks off a new lunar year. It’s celebrated by Chinese people of certain ethnic groups (the Han majority ethnicity). It’s also referred to as Spring Festival. The name was adopted in 1914 when China started following the Gregorian Calendar and celebrating the New Year on January 1.
block_08: Lunar New Year is the first day of the first month in any moon cycle-based calendar.
block_09: Simply put, the Chinese New Year = Spring Festival in China and the Lunar New Year are similar celebrations in other regions that follow the lunar calendar.
block_10: Lunar New Year is celebrated in:
block_23: Important: although the Lunar New Year is not an official holiday all over Asia, the Asian communities massively celebrate it globally. For them, it's a huge holiday that they'd never miss. If your users originate from one of the countries above, don’t hesitate to reach them with congrats.
block_27: Firecrackers and fireworks to scare away evil and bring good luck.
block_28: Red envelopes — the most common Lunar New Year gifts. They have money inside and are believed to bring good luck. People often use the WeChat red envelopes app.
block_29: Traditional red lanterns are believed to bring good luck and symbolize national pride.
block_31: All-things-red: flags, gift wrappings, dragons, envelopes, couplets pasted on the doors, and of course, the red ox — the symbol of the Lunar New Year 2021.
block_34: Glutinous rice cake (believed to bring a higher income or position.)
block_35: Fish as a symbol of prosperity (a must on the reunion dinner table.)
block_36: Happy Lunar New Year to everyone who celebrates!
